subject: [PATCH v4] mm: mglru: fix stale batch updates after memcg reparenting

reviews.author: Qi Zheng
reviews.summary: reviewer asked whether the implementation for !CONFIG_MEMCG should also be placed in the same location as the existing code, and mentioned that they will send a revised patch (v5) soon
reviews.raw_body:
All right, should the implementation for !CONFIG_MEMCG be placed here
too?

Will send the v5.

Thanks,
Qi

reviews.author: Johannes Weiner (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2 by adding a conditional #ifdef/#else to handle CONFIG_MEMCG

2026-07-10.patch_summary: This patch fixes an issue where stale batch updates occur after memcg reparenting in the mm:mglru code. The problem arises from the fact that the lruvec lock is not properly updated when a memory cgroup is reparented, leading to inconsistent state. The patch addresses this by updating the locking logic to ensure that the lruvec lock is correctly acquired and released during memcg reparenting.
